organ_toxicities.manifestation: Pulmonary fibrosis, pneumonitis, ARDS. Occurs in 5-15% of patients.
organ_toxicities.monitoring: Annual chest X-ray and PFTs. DLCO is most sensitive marker.
organ_toxicities.management: Discontinue amiodarone. Consider systemic corticosteroids for severe cases.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: Both hypothyroidism (6-10%) and hyperthyroidism (2-12%). Amiodarone contains 37% iodine by weight.
organ_toxicities.monitoring: TSH and free T4 every 6 months
organ_toxicities.management: Hypothyroidism: levothyroxine supplementation. Hyperthyroidism: may require steroids or thyroidectomy.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: Elevated transaminases (15-30%), hepatitis, cirrhosis (rare). Can be fatal.
organ_toxicities.monitoring: LFTs every 6 months. Discontinue if transaminases >3x ULN.
organ_toxicities.management: Discontinue amiodarone. Hepatotoxicity usually reversible if caught early.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: Corneal microdeposits (>90% of patients, usually asymptomatic), optic neuropathy (rare but sight-threatening)
organ_toxicities.management: Corneal deposits: usually resolve after discontinuation. Optic neuropathy: discontinue immediately.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: Photosensitivity (25-75%), blue-gray skin discoloration (4-9%) with prolonged use
organ_toxicities.management: Use high-SPF sunscreen. Discoloration may be permanent.

organ_toxicities.manifestation: Bradycardia, QT prolongation, torsades de pointes (rare), worsening heart failure
organ_toxicities.monitoring: Regular ECG monitoring, especially QTc interval
organ_toxicities.management: Dose reduction or discontinuation. Temporary pacing for severe bradycardia.
Usually resolves with dose reduction or discontinuation

overdose_management: Supportive care. No specific antidote. Cholestyramine may enhance elimination. Hemodialysis is NOT effective due to high protein binding and large volume of distribution.
